title: lychnis alba subsp. divaricata
intro: Lychnis alba subsp. divaricata, also known as White Campion or White Catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Lychnis alba subsp. divaricata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies and homeopathy.
